message:
fix(storage): exclude WAL/SHM sidecars from backup cleanup, remove dead analytics migrations

Backup cleanup (cleanup_old_backups):
- Added is_backup_root_name() helper that filters out -wal and -shm
  sidecar files when enumerating backups for rotation. Previously,
  each .db-wal and .db-shm file was counted as a separate backup
  entry, causing the rotation to delete real backups prematurely
  while leaving orphaned sidecars behind.
- Added test (cleanup_old_backups_ignores_wal_and_shm_sidecars) that
  creates backups with WAL+SHM sidecars and verifies only root files
  are counted for keep_count and sidecars follow their roots.

Storage open resilience:
- Added test (open_or_rebuild_does_not_treat_non_database_paths_as_corruption)
  verifying that passing a directory path to open_or_rebuild returns a
  Database or Io error rather than triggering a rebuild cycle.

Dead migration removal:
- Removed MIGRATION_V11 (message_metrics + usage_hourly + usage_daily
  analytics tables), MIGRATION_V12 (model dimensions + usage_models_daily),
  and MIGRATION_V13 (plan-attributed token rollups). These analytics tables
  were never populated by any code path and added unnecessary schema
  complexity. V14 (FTS5 contentless migration) is retained.

Minor:
- Removed unnecessary `mut` qualifiers on storage variables in tests
- Whitespace formatting adjustments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
